title: Deterministic Model Port, Scripted Fake, Recordings, and Test Identity · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent organization supplies a different user population, task distribution, tool set, risk tier, model response, resource budget, accessibility need, and failure condition and transfers deterministic model port, scripted fake, recordings, and test identity into a changed scripted provider fake and versioned interaction fixture set. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of scripted provider fake and versioned interaction fixture set with automated and judgment…
objectives: Express model requests, typed steps, usage, errors, cancellation, and async lifecycle through a minimal Python protocol.
Build a scripted fake that consumes expected requests and emits deterministic model output, tool calls, safety blocks, usage, and faults.
Store sanitized versioned provider fixtures with schema, model, API, SDK, timestamp, purpose, and explicit replay limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Model Protocol
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab agent engineering team handles case 6482 during a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review. Build deterministic Python evidence to define a provider port, scripted fake, versioned recordings, request identity, and deterministic no-live default; telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable; then inject cancellation, retry, repeated-call, checkpoint, or replay failure and recover without duplicate effects. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab No Live Default
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-no-live-default-vp5cpv" that uses case 1269 to define a provider port, scripted fake, versioned recordings, request identity, and deterministic no-live default. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns No Live Default into observable performance evidence. For Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab No Live Default, keep No Live Default connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-no-live-default-vp5cpv
# Competency: agentpy-no-live-default.
# Case 1269: a clean package, canary, and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: model output and tool arguments remain untrusted until validated.
# Changed case: replace trusted context with untrusted indirect instructions and preserve data-versus-instruction boundaries.
def evidence_agentpy_no_live_default_vp5cpv(scripted_turns: int = 18, replayed_turns: int = 18, fixture_versions: int = 3, leaked_live_calls: int = 0, unexplained_differences: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_vp5cpv = "vp5cpv-vpc5pv-1269"
    assert evidence_variant_vp5cpv
    evidence_axes_vp5cpv = (
        "authorityvp5cpv",
        "interaction1269",
        "toolvpc5pv",
        "statevp5cpv1269",
        "evaluation1269vp5cpv",
        "recoveryvpc5pv1269",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_vp5cpv)) == 6
    if scripted_turns < 8 or replayed_turns < 0:
        return False, replayed_turns, "fake-depth"
    if replayed_turns != scripted_turns:
        return False, replayed_turns, "replay-gap"
    if fixture_versions < 2 or unexplained_differences > 0:
        return False, fixture_versions, "fixture-drift"
    if leaked_live_calls > 0:
        return False, leaked_live_calls, "live-call-leak"
    assert replayed_turns == scripted_turns and leaked_live_calls == 0
    return True, replayed_turns, "deterministic-model-port"
